theme_and_archetype.theme: Dark Mode Default (with Light Mode toggle)
theme_and_archetype.archetype: Mix of Performance Pro & Vibrant Play
theme_and_archetype.rationale: High school and college students need a tool that feels fast, engaging, and non-clinical. The vibrant blue and orange create an energetic contrast against the deep dark background, turning studying into a 'command-center' experience rather than a chore.

typography.strategy: Clean, highly readable geometric sans-serif (System fonts: SF Pro on iOS, Roboto on Android) with distinct weight contrasts.

spacing_and_layout.strategy: Generous spacing. Avoid cramped screens. Use large touch targets.

buttons.primary: Solid #2979FF background, rounded-pill (9999px), generous height (min 56px) for easy tapping, centered bold text.
buttons.secondary: Solid #FF6D00 background or outlined, used for highly distinct actions like 'Start Pomodoro'.
buttons.action: Small icon-based buttons within cards. Use subtle surface_highlight backgrounds.

cards.style: Flat with crisp rounded corners (16px). In dark mode, rely on surface color contrast (#1E293B on #0F172A) rather than shadows. In light mode, use subtle shadows (elevation: 2, shadowOpacity: 0.05).
cards.layout: Flexbox, standard gaps (16px), right-aligned action icons.

inputs.style: Large tap areas (min-height 50px), filled background (surface_highlight), subtle border on focus, 12px border radius.

navigation.bottom_tabs: Minimalist. Hide labels if possible or keep them tiny. Highlight active tab with #2979FF tint and a small dot indicator below the icon.

charts.recommendation: Use react-native-chart-kit or react-native-gifted-charts. Ensure charts use the primary (#2979FF) and secondary (#FF6D00) colors. Hide grid lines for a cleaner, modern look. Use bezier curves for line charts.

Dashboard.layout: Vertical scroll. Top: Greeting & Motivation. Middle: Weekly Study Hours Chart. Bottom: Weak Subjects (Bento-style grid) and Quick Action floating buttons.

Pomodoro_Timer.layout: Centered massive timer display. High contrast. H1 typography. Subject selector dropdown above the timer. 'Start/Pause' massive pill button below.

Error_Scanner.layout: Camera viewfinder simulation taking up top 60% of screen. 'Capture' button and 'Gallery' icon. Bottom sheet for mock AI analysis loading state and auto-fill form.
Error_Scanner.visuals: Use futuristic neon styling for the scanner overlay (reticle, scanning line animation).

AI_Reports.layout: Data-heavy but clean. Tab toggle for 'Weekly' / 'Monthly'. Bar/Line charts for mistake trends. Scrollable list of 'AI Recommended Study Plan' below.

image_urls.description: Student studying, use as header background or motivation card.

testing.directives: All interactive elements, buttons, inputs, and major views MUST include a `data-testid` attribute (e.g., `data-testid="start-pomodoro-button"`).

instructions_to_main_agent: Use standard React Native components (View, Text, TouchableOpacity, ScrollView).
Use Expo Vector Icons (e.g., Ionicons, Feather) for all iconography.
For the theme toggle, use a simple React Context holding 'light' or 'dark' state.
Mock all data locally (study hours, weak subjects, AI insights). No backend required.
Use StyleSheet.create for all styling, referencing the color palette defined here.
Ensure the Pomodoro timer actually counts down and manages the 25m/5m states.
Simulate the Error Scanner using a View with a border overlay, and a setTimeout to mock the 'AI analyzing...' state before showing form results.
